rationale: In the theory development, the cited paper is used to support a specific claim that interdependent self-construal increases actions that benefit the collective over individual self-interest. That claim is central to the citing paper's mechanism and hypothesis logic (crowdfunding activates interdependence, which raises demand for social-good products). The citation therefore does more than provide generic background, even though the new paper tests the idea in a different transaction context.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its core conceptual argument on Winterich and Barone (2011) as prior evidence linking interdependent self-construal to prosocial/collective-oriented consumer choices. It then uses that foundation to motivate predictions about crowdfunding versus purchase transactions.
